headline.text: Two metal foreign-material hits at L1 — S3 caught metal in depositor product, S2 caught *washer* in pouch — both investigated, no holds required under 3-hit policy.

summary: Two active FM investigations at L1. S3 depositor line metal event at 12:44 resolved after scrapping tunnel 1 and clearing L2 grinder. S2 washer detected at 19:26, equipment inspected, source unconfirmed but within policy threshold. Salt contamination cleaned from S2 tunnel 3. L3 ML1 open-bar spike (6%) resolved overnight through speed reduction.

food_safety.title: Metal foreign material in S3 depositor product
food_safety.summary: QC found metal in finished product rejected by metal detector at 12:44; maintenance traced to cereal ingredient, scrapped tunnel 1 product, inspected all scrapers/rollers/bearings and L2 grinding blades—all clear.
food_safety.status: Resolved — line restarted 13:35 after equipment inspection and product removal; L2 grinder cleared by maintenance.

food_safety.title: Washer detected in S2 pouch at X-ray
food_safety.summary: X-ray rejected pouch containing washer at 19:26 (pouch timestamp 19:14); line was already down for belt maintenance, all accumulation bins and equipment inspected, no missing hardware or source identified.
food_safety.status: Open investigation — first hit in 8-hour window, no hold required per 3-hit policy; source of washer not yet confirmed.

food_safety.title: Salt contamination in S2 tunnel 3
food_safety.summary: Tunnel 3 began dropping salt at line startup 08:05, rollers and tunnel interior covered; production stopped, tunnel and rollers cleaned, QA inspected at 09:13 but salt remained; second clean performed.
food_safety.status: Resolved after second cleaning cycle; tunnel released for production.

food_safety.title: ML1 metal detector foot (plastic puck) missing
food_safety.summary: One of four leveling risers on metal detector missing at 13:35; detector standing on three legs only, affecting belt tracking stability.
food_safety.status: Open — 2.5" × 2.5" plastic puck not located; belt tracking issues noted.

food_safety.title: ML4 mold-return component repeatedly falling out
food_safety.summary: Random piece inside mold return at 18:00 on 18th; production reports it tends to fall out periodically and is replaced each time.
food_safety.status: Open recurring issue — component replaced but root cause not addressed; potential FM risk if enters product path.

food_safety.title: ML4 TG3 cleaning roller missing scraper blade
food_safety.summary: Roller on TG3 found without scraper at shift start 06:42; no indication of when blade was lost or whether fragment entered product zone.
food_safety.status: Open — scraper location unknown, no product hold initiated.

quality.title: S2 box printer timestamp mismatch between sides
quality.summary: West-side print head printing incorrect time vs. east side at 13:34; VideoJet software 15 versions behind (known clock-sync bug), cases bypassed to rework stack until independent-printer workaround applied.
quality.status: Resolved via workaround — borrowed S3 jar-line Leader printer to run both printers independently; software upgrade scheduled.

quality.title: ML1 flow-wrap open-unit rate spike (35g bars)
quality.summary: Master-case inspection at 23:08 found 6% open bars (east-side Cavanna dominant); line slowed from 18 to 15 MPM, rate dropped to 2% by 02:46 and 0% by 05:38.
quality.status: Resolved — speed reduction and process adjustments corrected seal quality; troubleshooting ongoing to ramp speed back up.

quality.title: MF Matrix checkweigher rejecting 31-piece bags (target 32)
quality.summary: Bags rejecting at checkweigh with one-piece underweight at 07:41; maintenance on line adjusting.

quality.title: ML1 flow-wrap open-unit rate on 12g bars
quality.summary: Box inspection at 08:03 found 9 open units out of 1,050 (0.8% open rate); informational flag ahead of 35g run.
quality.status: Noted — rate within tolerance, same Cavanna line later showed higher issue on 35g.

sanitation.title: S2 mini tempering unit pipes — residue and odor
sanitation.summary: Discolored chocolate from tempering unit at 08:02; pipes removed, residue with unpleasant odor found inside (highlighted in photo); maintenance confirmed from oil-cleaning cycle, purged with chocolate until clean.
sanitation.status: Resolved — pipes purged and restored to service after oil-cleaning residue cleared.

opportunities.text: Consider adding loose-hardware inspection (washers, nuts, small parts) to pre-shift and post-maintenance checklists — based on S2 washer FM where source could not be traced to specific maintenance activity.
Review metal-detector leveling-foot retention and add to ML1 pre-shift inspection — based on today's missing plastic puck that affects belt tracking and detector stability.
Add cleaning-roller scraper presence to ML4 startup checklist — based on TG3 missing scraper found at shift start with no record of when or where blade was lost.
